channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump taps immigration hard-liner Kari Lake as head of Voice of America
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he’s picking Kari Lake as director of Voice of America, installing a staunch loyalist who ran unsuccessfully for Arizona governor and a Senate seat to head the congressionally funded broadcaster that provides independent news reporting around the world.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump announces more nominations, including Kari Lake as director of Voice of America broadcast
item.description: President-elect Trump nominated Kari Lake to be director of the Voice of America, and Dr. Peter Lamelas and Daniel Newlin as ambassadors to Argentina and Colombia, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gov. Greg Abbott signals support for David Cook over Dustin Burrows in GOP fight for House speaker
item.description: Days after the Texas House GOP caucus backed Cook, Abbott made clear that he expects Republican representatives to do the same.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Juan Salinas Ii And Renzo Downey, KPRC 2

item.title: Person accused of accosting Rep. Nancy Mace at Capitol pleads not guilty to assault charge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A person accused of accosting U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace in a Capitol Office building pleaded not guilty on Wednesday to a misdemeanor assault charge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Secretary of State Blinken is returning to the Mideast in his latest diplomatic foray
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken is returning to the Middle East this week on his 12th visit since the Israel-Hamas war erupted last year but his first since the ouster of Syrian President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House approves $895B defense bill with military pay raise, ban on transgender care for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House on Wednesday passed a $895 billion measure that authorizes a 1% increase in defense spending this fiscal year and would give a double-digit pay raise to about half of the enlisted service members in the military.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House small business panel releases year-end report on 'partisan' Biden agency electioneering allegations
item.description: The House Small Business Committee released its long-awaited report on the SBA's memo of understanding with the Michigan Dept of State over voter registration and alleged partisan electioneering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries says he's 'prepared to find common ground' with Trump next year
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries said addressing the high cost of living for Americans could be an area of bipartisan cooperation next year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: "It was a pleasure to have dinner the other night with Governor Justin Trudeau of the Great State of Canada," Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Rising US-Mexico tension a game of optics, analyst says
item.description: Change is coming to the border on Jan. 20, when President-elect Trump takes office. How profound and lasting such change might be remains to be seen, but one thing is certain: Optics will play a big role in Washington, D.C., and in Mexico City.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Judge considering delay request in Routh attempted assassination trial
item.description: Attorneys for Ryan Routh, accused of attempting to assassinate Donald Trump, are requesting a 10-month trial delay
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth makes play to win hard-to-get GOP centrists
item.description: Pete Hegseth is looking to win over a pair of key Republican moderates as he tries to keep his hopes of leading the Defense Department from being derailed. The former Fox News host huddled with Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-Alaska) on Tuesday and is set to do the same on Wednesday with Sen.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle gets Greece as consolation prize after Donald Trump Jr. dumps her: Source
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle has been tapped to be the U.S. Ambassador to Greece in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Sanders says this will likely be his last term
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.), one of the Senate’s most prominent progressives, says he likely won’t run again for the Senate after his fourth term expires in 2030.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: James Comer predicts Biden will pardon his brother Jim
item.description: “I do think he’ll pardon other family members. He’s going to have to,” Comer said in the clip highlighted by Grabien.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Bragg lays out alternatives to dismissing Trump's hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg’s (D) office opposes dismissing President-elect Trump’s hush money charges but would accept halting the proceedings while he is in office, according to court documents made public Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Kash Patel FBI plans spark concern over agency independence, retribution
item.description: Kash Patel hasn’t offered specifics on his plans for the FBI, but he’s made it clear that sweeping changes are on the table.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Pelosi says she backs generational change on committees, but there are exceptions
item.description: Rep. Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) said this week that she supports the push for younger Democrats to replace veteran lawmakers at the top of committees — but there is at least one glaring exception to the rule.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Patel FBI plans spark concern over agency independence, retribution
item.description: Kash Patel, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the FBI, has promised to deliver a massive reform of a bureau he sees as among the agencies that have “weaponized the government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump says tariffs will not hurt Americans' pockets
item.description: Trump was unwilling to speculate whether the costs of tariffs would be passed on to the American consumer.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bidders are back in court battling over the auction of Alex Jones' Infowars
item.description: A U.S. bankruptcy judge is hearing arguments for and against selling the show to The Onion, the satirical news site named the winning bidder. Host Alex Jones says the auction was rigged.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects challenge to Boston's school admissions policy
item.description: The court's action was the second time the justices declined to intervene in an admissions program based on geography since their 2023 ruling invalidating affirmative action in higher education.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Minneapolis City Council’s budget committee reduces proposed property tax levy for 2025
item.description: Council members, citing concerns about elevated costs for constituents, approved a series of amendments to the mayor’s proposed budget which would reduce the levy hike by 1.4 percent.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump’s position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC that focused on advertising likening Donald Trump’s stance on abortion to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates Rodney Scott to lead Customs and Border Protection
item.description: Rodney Scott at CBP and Caleb Vitello at ICE would work alongside Stephen Miller, who was named deputy chief of staff for policy and Tom Homan, also tapped to be a "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: NYC plan would abolish income tax for certain residents: See if you qualify
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A new proposal would abolish New York City’s personal income tax for nearly half a million qualifying residents.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy are bringing Trump’s DOGE to Capitol Hill
item.description: The leaders of the so-called “Department of Government Efficiency” are calling for large-scale layoffs of federal workers and the elimination of some federal agencies during Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
